nativeRange.habitat: Deep coastal drop-offs and dead-reef areas in somewhat turbid water, typically at depths of 40-54 m (broader range 20-100 m).

decorPreferences: live rock with caves and overhangs

diet.notes: Active planktivore in the wild. In captivity, generally accepts frozen mysis shrimp and enriched frozen brine shrimp, and over time may take high-quality flake foods. Multiple small feedings per day are recommended, and an attached refugium cultivating copepods and amphipods is widely advised to keep this species in long-term condition.

avoidWith: other Pseudanthias males in cramped quarters
very boisterous tankmates that outcompete it for food

summary: A deep-reef Indo-Pacific anthias generally kept in a male-with-multiple-females harem; needs a large, well-fed system with plenty of swimming space and several daily feedings.
careNotes: The Bimaculatus Anthias is a mesophotic-reef planktivore generally considered intermediate in care: cool, well-oxygenated water, ample open swimming space, and several small feedings per day are commonly advised. Like other Pseudanthias it is a protogynous (female-first) sequential hermaphrodite that naturally forms harems of one dominant male with several females; if the male is lost, the largest female has been reported to transition to male. Best kept as a harem of 1 male + 3-5 females rather than singly; in mixed groups, males tend to be aggressive toward other males. Anthias are well-known jumpers and a tightly sealed lid is generally recommended. Cross-source note: LiveAquaria lists an adult size of ~13 cm while FishBase records a max length of 14 cm TL, so the 10-14 cm range used here brackets both. LiveAquaria advises a minimum of ~125-130 US gallons (~475-490 L); given this species' active mid-water swimming and need for a male to hold a territory above the rockwork, the recommendation has been rounded up to 500 L with a 150 cm long footprint.
breedingNotes: Not routinely bred in home aquaria. The species is a protogynous hermaphrodite and broadcast spawner; closely related Pseudanthias spp. (e.g., P. pleurotaenia) have been reported to spawn at the same time and occasionally produce hybrids in the wild.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Cross-checked taxonomy, distribution, depth/temperature range, and max length. FishBase max length 14.0 cm TL is larger than LiveAquaria's stated ~13 cm; size range broadened to 10-14 cm. FishBase preferred temperature 25.6-28.4 C is warmer than LiveAquaria's 22.2-25.6 C; final range 23-27 C averages the two.
